title: sorghum bicolor nothosubsp. drummondii
intro: Sorghum bicolor nothosubsp. drummondii, also known as broomcorn, is a subspecies of sorghum that is primarily cultivated for its inflorescences, which are used to make brooms. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of sorghum bicolor nothosubsp. drummondii include its ability to help lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and provide relief from fever and rheumatism. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical treatments.
